questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A bead under the influence of gravity, slides down a frictionless wire whose $y$ coordinate is changing with $x$ co-ordinate as shown in the figure. Assume that at position O the wire is vertical and the bead passes this point with a given speed $\mathrm{v}_0$ downward. If the shape of the wire is such that the vertical component of velocity remains $\mathrm{v}_0$ at all time, find $(a+b+c)$ in the shape function of wire given by $y=\frac{\left(\operatorname{agv}_0 \mathrm{x}\right)^{\frac{b}{c}}}{2 g}$, where g is gravitational acceleration.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & v=\sqrt{v_0^2+2 g y} \\ & \text { and } v \sin \theta=v_0 \text { (as given in question) } \\ & \therefore \sin \theta=\frac{v_0}{\sqrt{v_0^2+2 g y}} \\ & \therefore \tan \theta=\frac{v_0}{\sqrt{2 g y}} \\ & \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{v_0}{\sqrt{2 g y}} \\ & \therefore y=\frac{\left(3 g v_0 x\right)^{2 / 3}}{2 g} \\ & \therefore a+b+c=8\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A long plank begins to move at $t=0$ and accelerates along a straight track with a speed given by $v=2 t^2$ for $0 \leq t \leq 2$ (where $v$ is in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $t$ is in second). After 2 sec the plank continues to move at the constant speed acquired. A small block initially at rest on the plank begins to slip at $\mathrm{t}=1 \mathrm{sec}$ and stops sliding at $\mathrm{t}=3 \mathrm{sec}$. If the coefficient of static friction and kinetic friction between the plank and the block is $0 . \mathrm{s}$ and $0 . \mathrm{k}$ (where s and k are digits) respectively, find $\mathrm{s}+\mathrm{k}$. $\left(\right.$ take $\left.\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2\right)$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mu_{\mathrm{s}} \mathrm{~g}=\frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dt}} \text { at } \mathrm{t}=1 \mathrm{sec} \\
& \therefore \mu_{\mathrm{s}}=0.4
\end{aligned}$

At $t=3 \mathrm{sec}$ velocities of plank and block are equal.
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore 2+\mu_{\mathrm{k}} \mathrm{~g}(2)=\mathrm{v} \text { at } \mathrm{t}=3 \mathrm{sec}=8 \\
& \therefore \mu_{\mathrm{k}}=0.3 \\
& \therefore \mathrm{~s}+\mathrm{k}=4+3=7
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A square carpet of a mass of 20 kg is dragged from one room into another as shown in the figure. The width of the corridor and the carpet is the same, 2 m . The friction coefficient is 0.1 at the end of the first room and 0.2 at the other end, in the corridor it changes linearly from 0.1 to 0.2 . The carpet presses the floor uniformly. If the amount of work that must be done to drag the carpet slowly from the first room to the other is x Joules. Find the value of $x$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle moves with a tangential acceleration $a_t=\vec{a} \cdot \hat{v}$ where $\vec{a}=(5 \hat{i}) \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2$. If the speed of the particle is zero at $\mathrm{x}=0$, then find $\mathrm{v}(\mathrm{in} \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s})$ at $\mathrm{x}=4.9 \mathrm{~m}$.
questions.solution: As $v d v=\vec{a} \cdot \overline{d r}=a d x=5 d x \Rightarrow \int_0^v v d v=5 \int_0^{4.9} d x \Rightarrow \frac{v^2}{2}=5(4.9) \Rightarrow \mathrm{v}^2=49 \Rightarrow \mathrm{v}=7 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A path is made to a uniform density cube of mass $m$, and of edge a, such that when the cube rolls along the path without skidding, its centre moves along a straight line. At the top of the path a horizontal velocity of center of mass is $\mathrm{v}_0$ and the cube is in pure rolling. The static frictional force is big enough, so the cube do not slide anywhere. The speed of the centre of mass when the cube touches the lowest point of the path is $\frac{\sqrt{\mathrm{K}} \mathrm{v}_0}{2}$ ? (The moment of inertia of the cube with respect to its centre of mass is $\mathrm{ma}^2 / 6$ ). Find k .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle of mass 2 kg moves on a smooth horizontal plane under the action of a single force $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}=(30 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+40 \hat{\mathrm{j}}) \mathrm{N}$. Under this force it is displaced from $(0,0)$ to $(100 \mathrm{~m}, 100 \mathrm{~m})$. If the initial speed of the particle is $\sqrt{3000} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ then find out its final speed in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A block of mass 2 kg is tied to elastic thread of length 2 m and suspended to point O. Initially block is horizontal level to this point $O$. Now block is released and its speed is $5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ when thread is vertical. If the acceleration of the block at the lowest position is 2.5 K . Find the value of K .
questions.solution:
M.E. Conservation
$\begin{aligned}
& m g(4)-\frac{k}{2} x^2=\frac{1}{2} m v^2 \\
& k x=55 \\
& -m g+k x=m a \\
& 55-20=2 a \\
& a=17.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}^2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A small ball of mass 100 g is attached to a light and inextensible string of length 50 cm . The string is tied to a support O and the mass m released from point A which is at a horizontal distance of 30 cm from the support. Calculate the speed of the ball at its lowest point of the trajectory. (mark the answer in $\mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$)
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{mg} \times 0.4=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2 \\
& \mathrm{v}=2 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}
\end{aligned}$
(from conservation of mechanical energy just before the string becomes taut) during the application of impulse, velocity along perpendicular to the string remain same. So after impulse velocity of the ball equal to $\frac{6 \sqrt{2}}{5} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ again from conservation of mechanical energy
$\frac{1}{2} m\left(\frac{6 \sqrt{2}}{5}\right)^2+m g(0.1)=\frac{1}{2} m v_f^2$

Now velocity at the bottommost point $\mathrm{v}_{\text {final }}=2.2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
